title: Frontend Developer Starter Kit
shortDescription: A polished self-learning path for HTML, CSS, JavaScript, responsive UI, and portfolio-ready practice.
description: Start from the foundations and build real interface confidence. This self-paced course is structured for learners who want clean HTML, responsive CSS, JavaScript interaction, and practical portfolio exercises.
imageAlt: Learner building a frontend project on a laptop
certificate: Certificate after recorded progress and final assessment
studyMaterial: Downloadable notes, code checklists, UI practice sheets, and final project prompts.
technologyRequirements: Laptop or desktop, modern browser, VS Code, and stable internet connection.
eligibilityRequirements: No prior programming experience required. Basic computer comfort is enough.
disclaimer: Learner outcome depends on regular practice and completion of assigned projects.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: Web Foundations
chapters.description: Understand browser basics, page structure, assets, semantic HTML, and project setup.

lectures.title: How websites are built
lectures.description: A practical introduction to pages, assets, links, developer tools, and deployment thinking.

lectures.title: Semantic HTML starter project
lectures.description: Create a clean page structure with headings, sections, forms, links, and media.

chapters.title: Responsive Interface Design
chapters.description: Build layouts that stay readable on mobile, tablet, and desktop screens.

lectures.title: CSS layout essentials
lectures.description: Use spacing, typography, flexbox, grid, and reusable layout patterns.

lectures.title: Mobile-first landing page
lectures.description: Turn a simple design into a responsive landing page with polished buttons and cards.

chapters.title: JavaScript for Real UI
chapters.description: Add meaningful interaction using events, DOM updates, validation, and loading states.

lectures.title: DOM events and state
lectures.description: Build interactive menus, tabs, counters, and small interface patterns.

lectures.title: Form validation mini project
lectures.description: Create a practical form flow with validation messages and submit feedback.

title: UI UX Design Essentials
shortDescription: A self-paced design course covering UX research, wireframes, design systems, and handoff basics.
description: Learn how to think like a product designer. This course covers user journeys, wireframes, visual hierarchy, accessible interface patterns, and developer handoff.
imageAlt: Designer reviewing a digital interface prototype
certificate: Certificate after design project submission and final quiz
studyMaterial: UX worksheets, design review checklist, wireframe templates, and handoff notes.
technologyRequirements: Laptop or desktop, Figma or browser-based design tool, and internet access.
eligibilityRequirements: No design degree required. Suitable for students, founders, and junior designers.
disclaimer: Design portfolio quality depends on project effort and feedback iteration.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: UX Thinking
chapters.description: Learn problem framing, user goals, customer journeys, and simple research notes.

lectures.title: Understanding users
lectures.description: Map user goals, pain points, and decisions before designing screens.

lectures.title: Journey map practice
lectures.description: Create a simple journey map and identify moments where UI can reduce friction.

chapters.title: Interface Craft
chapters.description: Improve hierarchy, spacing, typography, colors, components, and responsive composition.

lectures.title: Visual hierarchy basics
lectures.description: Use size, contrast, grouping, and alignment to make screens easier to scan.

lectures.title: Component consistency
lectures.description: Build repeatable buttons, cards, forms, and navigation patterns.

title: Digital Marketing Launchpad
shortDescription: A self-learning course for content planning, SEO basics, campaign funnels, and analytics reports.
description: This practical marketing course helps learners understand content strategy, search visibility, paid campaign planning, email sequences, and analytics reporting.
imageAlt: Marketing dashboard with campaign planning notes
certificate: Certificate after campaign plan submission and assessment
studyMaterial: Campaign planner, SEO checklist, content calendar template, and analytics worksheet.
technologyRequirements: Browser, spreadsheet tool, and access to basic social media or website examples.
eligibilityRequirements: Suitable for students, small business owners, creators, and marketing beginners.
disclaimer: Marketing results vary by niche, budget, offer, and execution quality.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: Marketing Foundations
chapters.description: Understand audiences, positioning, offers, channels, and conversion goals.

lectures.title: Audience and offer clarity
lectures.description: Define who the campaign is for, what problem it solves, and why it matters.

lectures.title: Channel selection
lectures.description: Choose the right mix of search, social, email, and content channels.

chapters.title: Campaign Execution
chapters.description: Plan content, landing pages, lead magnets, paid promotion, and email follow-up.

lectures.title: Content calendar workflow
lectures.description: Build a practical publishing calendar with topics, owners, and outcomes.

lectures.title: Funnel and email sequence
lectures.description: Create a simple campaign funnel from awareness to lead capture and conversion.

title: Ayurveda Nutrition Self-Learning Program
shortDescription: A self-learning wellness course with recorded lessons, downloadable worksheets, and certificate readiness.
description: This self-learning wellness program demonstrates recorded lessons, downloadable planning sheets, practical food routines, progress tracking, and completion certificates.
imageAlt: Ayurveda nutrition course with healthy food planning
certificate: Certificate after lesson completion and worksheet practice
studyMaterial: Printable diet planning sheets, case discussion templates, and recorded lesson references.
technologyRequirements: Mobile or laptop, modern browser, PDF reader, and stable internet.
eligibilityRequirements: Open to wellness learners, yoga trainers, nutrition enthusiasts, and healthcare assistants.
disclaimer: This course is educational and does not replace medical diagnosis or clinical treatment.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: Foundations of Ayurvedic Nutrition
chapters.description: Recorded lessons cover prakriti, digestion, meal timing, food qualities, and practical routines.

chapters.title: Diet Planning Workshops
chapters.description: Learners practice meal plans, seasonal adjustments, and simple case examples.

chapters.title: Case Review and Completion
chapters.description: Learners review common client scenarios, revise key concepts, and complete the course.

title: Spoken English Confidence Builder
shortDescription: A self-learning spoken English course for fluency, interview practice, and confidence building.
description: This course demonstrates how language academies can sell recorded speaking lessons with prompts, practice tasks, progress tracking, and completion certificates.
imageAlt: Learners practicing spoken English with recorded lessons
certificate: Certificate after lesson completion and speaking practice tasks
studyMaterial: Speaking prompts, vocabulary sheets, interview scripts, and weekly practice tasks.
technologyRequirements: Mobile or laptop, microphone for practice recording, PDF reader, and stable internet.
eligibilityRequirements: Learners should be comfortable reading basic English sentences.
disclaimer: Fluency improves with regular self-practice, revision, and speaking confidence.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: Confidence and Pronunciation
chapters.description: Recorded speaking drills, pronunciation practice, and confidence-building exercises.

chapters.title: Daily Conversation Practice
chapters.description: Roleplays for introductions, workplace conversations, calls, and common situations.

chapters.title: Interview and Presentation Practice
chapters.description: Mock interviews, short presentations, self-review prompts, and final practice tasks.

title: Full Stack Web Development Blueprint
shortDescription: A self-learning full stack program with recorded modules, project briefs, resources, and certificates.
description: This premium starter course combines recorded lessons, project briefs, downloadable resources, progress tracking, certificates, and learner dashboards for a clean self-learning flow.
certificate: Certificate after lesson completion and project practice
studyMaterial: Recorded modules, Git workflow guide, project briefs, and review checklists.
technologyRequirements: Laptop or desktop, Node.js, VS Code, Git, modern browser, and internet.
eligibilityRequirements: Basic computer skills required. Prior coding experience is helpful but not mandatory.
disclaimer: Project readiness depends on completing the recorded lessons and project practice.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: Recorded Foundations
chapters.description: Self-paced modules prepare learners for practical project building.

lectures.title: Development environment setup
lectures.description: Install and configure Node.js, VS Code, Git, browser tools, and project folders.

lectures.title: JavaScript and TypeScript essentials
lectures.description: Revise variables, functions, async flows, types, and practical debugging habits.

chapters.title: Frontend Application Build
chapters.description: Build authenticated screens, dashboards, forms, tables, and reusable UI patterns.

lectures.title: Component planning
lectures.description: Break screens into maintainable components, states, and data-fetching boundaries.

lectures.title: Forms and validation
lectures.description: Create polished form flows with validation, loading states, and helpful errors.

title: Data Analytics Career Starter
shortDescription: A self-learning analytics course with recorded Excel/SQL modules, dashboards, and portfolio practice.
description: This analytics track is designed for learners who need structured recorded practice, datasets, dashboard templates, and portfolio-ready reporting exercises.
imageAlt: Data analytics dashboard and learner notes
certificate: Certificate after lesson completion and analytics portfolio practice
studyMaterial: Practice datasets, dashboard templates, SQL exercises, and case study briefs.
technologyRequirements: Laptop or desktop, spreadsheet software, browser, SQL practice tool, and stable internet.
eligibilityRequirements: Suitable for students, analysts, operations teams, and career switchers.
disclaimer: Portfolio quality depends on completed practice assignments and review participation.
exams: No assessment required for starter demo courses

chapters.title: Analytics Foundations
chapters.description: Learn data cleaning, metrics, spreadsheet logic, and practical analysis habits.

lectures.title: Data thinking for beginners
lectures.description: Understand rows, columns, metrics, dimensions, and business questions.

lectures.title: Spreadsheet cleanup workflow
lectures.description: Practice cleaning messy data and preparing it for charts and reports.

chapters.title: SQL and Reporting
chapters.description: Use SQL queries and reporting patterns to answer practical business questions.

lectures.title: SQL select and filters
lectures.description: Write simple queries, filter records, sort outputs, and avoid common mistakes.

lectures.title: Report storytelling
lectures.description: Turn metrics into clear observations, risks, and recommendations.
